title: National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration
subtitle: The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.
caption: Across the globe in 2021, national migration policies show a wide range of alignment with international standards for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration, as measured by the United Nations. The map uses a five-color scale to show whether countries fully meet criteria, meet criteria, partially meet criteria, require further progress, or have no data available. Countries that fully meet criteria appear in dark blue and include Peru, South Africa, and Iran. Light blue indicates countries that meet criteria, a category that covers most of Europe — including France, Germany, and Sweden — as well as Canada, Australia, and Argentina. Orange marks nations that partially meet criteria, such as the United States, Brazil, India, China, and Morocco. Red signals countries requiring further progress, visible in Yemen, Sudan, and Myanmar. Gray hatching denotes no data, covering Greenland, Western Sahara, and several small island states. The distribution shows no single continent dominating any one category; instead, performance varies within regions. In Africa, for example, South Africa and Morocco fall into different bands, while in Asia, Iran and India are separated by two levels on the scale. Europe stands out for having the highest concentration of countries in the top two categories, with nearly all nations colored light or dark blue. The Americas show mixed results: North America splits between orange and light blue, South America includes both dark blue and orange, and Central America leans toward orange and red. The most visible pattern is the clustering of high-performing countries in western and northern Europe, contrasted with scattered low performers across parts of Africa, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia.
